title: Ingest specs into memory after planning writes
description: Hook planner persistence so a successful `takt plan --write` ingests the spec into the `specs` namespace and logs non-fatal failures without aborting planning.
acceptance_criteria: Planner write flow attempts spec ingestion only after successful bead persistence.
Spec ingestion targets namespace `specs` with source `planner`.
Failures are logged as warnings and do not abort planning.
The implementation reuses the memory ingestion backend and project-root DB path conventions.

handoff_summary.completed: Modified `PlanningService.write_plan` to accept optional `spec_path` and call `_ingest_spec` after all bead persistence. Added `_ingest_spec` helper that calls `ingest_file(..., namespace="specs", source="planner")` with non-fatal error handling. Updated CLI `command_plan` to pass `spec_path=spec_path` to `write_plan`.
handoff_summary.risks: The `ingest_file` call runs synchronously in the planner write flow, so slow model downloads or DB operations will delay `takt plan --write` output. The memory DB must already exist (created by `takt init`) — if it doesn't, the failure is logged as a warning and planning succeeds anyway.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: (1) `write_plan` with a real spec path and an initialised DB ingests chunks into namespace `specs` with source `planner`; (2) `write_plan` with a missing DB still returns created bead IDs without raising; (3) `write_plan` without `spec_path` behaves identically to the old signature; (4) the early-return (no feature) path also triggers ingestion when `spec_path` is given.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only `planner.py` and `misc.py` were changed; no overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Added `spec_path` as a keyword-only parameter to `write_plan` (backward-compatible — existing callers and tests pass zero arguments and continue to work). Ingestion is a post-persistence side-effect rather than a separate public method so it stays in the natural write flow. Restructured the early-return (`proposal.feature is None`) into an `if` block so the ingestion call at the bottom fires regardless of whether a feature tree was built. `_ingest_spec` is private (`_` prefix) to signal it is an implementation detail of `write_plan`, not part of the public API. DB path follows the established `{storage.root}/.takt/memory/memory.db` convention used in `runner.py` and `onboarding/scaffold.py`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should add: (1) a test that mocks or stubs `ingest_file` and asserts it is called with `namespace="specs"` and `source="planner"` when `spec_path` is supplied; (2) a test that makes `ingest_file` raise and verifies `write_plan` still returns the created bead list (non-fatal); (3) a test without `spec_path` asserting `ingest_file` is NOT called; (4) verify the no-feature-tree branch also triggers ingestion. Existing tests in `test_planner.py` exercise the bead-graph path and should not be affected since they do not pass `spec_path`.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Ingestion runs synchronously; for very large specs it may add latency to `takt plan --write`. If the memory DB has never been initialised (`takt init` not run), ingestion fails silently (warning only). The deduplication threshold in `ingest_file` means repeated `takt plan --write` on the same spec will skip already-similar chunks — this is intentional idempotency behaviour inherited from the memory backend.

conflict_risks: None — only `planner.py` and `misc.py` were changed; no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Hooked planner persistence to ingest the spec file into memory after a successful `takt plan --write`. Modified `PlanningService.write_plan` to accept a keyword-only `spec_path` parameter and call the new `_ingest_spec` helper after all beads are saved. The helper uses `ingest_file` from the memory backend, targets namespace `specs` with source `planner`, derives the DB path as `{storage.root}/.takt/memory/memory.db`, and wraps the call in a broad try/except that logs failures as warnings without aborting planning. Updated the CLI `command_plan` to pass `spec_path` when calling `write_plan`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
